stories.name: leave-sync-module
stories.title: Add leaveSync.ts with kind constant, payload type, and serialize/parse helpers
stories.description: Create app/src/lib/marmot/leaveSync.ts as a pure-function module with no React or IDB imports. Exports LEAVE_INTENT_KIND = 13, the LeaveIntentPayload interface, serialiseLeaveIntent, and parseLeaveIntent. Mirrors pollSync.ts. Includes unit tests for round-trip and parse-failure cases.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S1 creates a new file (leaveSync.ts does not exist on disk); new_file = true forces lighter_path = false.

stories.name: leave-handler-dispatch
stories.title: Add leaveHandler.ts and register kind-13 in buildDispatcher()
stories.description: Create app/src/lib/marmot/handlers/leaveHandler.ts exporting LeaveHandlerDeps and createLeaveIntentHandler following the pollHandler.ts pattern. Extend HandlerDeps in registerHandlers.ts to include enqueueLeave and append createLeaveIntentHandler to the handlers array. Wire a stub enqueueLeave in MarmotContext as a placeholder until S4 provides the real closure. AR-1 and AR-3 invariants must continue to hold. Unit-tested with vi.fn deps.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S2 creates a new file (leaveHandler.ts does not exist on disk) and modifies code files; new_file = true and files_code non-empty.

introduces_mocks.description: Stub enqueueLeave callback wired into buildDispatcher — no-op placeholder until S4 supplies the real MarmotContext closure

stories.name: send-path-leave-intent
stories.title: leaveGroup emits kind-13 leave intent and kind-9 fire-and-forget announcement before local purge
stories.description: Replace the soft-mute no-op inside leaveGroup() in MarmotContext.tsx with: (a) kind-13 sendApplicationRumor carrying LeaveIntentPayload, (b) fire-and-forget kind-9 chat rumor with {type:'leave_intent', pubkey}, (c) existing local-state purge sequence, (d) navigate to /groups. Send order: kind-13 must precede kind-9 and the purge. If kind-13 fails due to unapplied proposals, fall back to sendRumorSafe before retrying. mlsGroup.leave() must not be called.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S3 modifies MarmotContext.tsx, a code file; files_code is non-empty.

stories.name: pending-removal-queue
stories.title: Pending-removal queue and 5-second debounce in MarmotContext
stories.description: Add pendingRemovalsRef (Map<groupId, PendingRemoval[]>) and debounceTimersRef (Map<groupId, NodeJS.Timeout>) as useRef (not useState) in MarmotContext. Implement the enqueueLeave(groupId, pubkey) closure that appends a PendingRemoval and arms/resets the per-group 5s debounce timer. Replace the stub enqueueLeave from S2 with the real closure injected into buildDispatcher via deps.enqueueLeave. Clear timers in the groupSubsRef cleanup at MarmotContext.tsx:720-727. No commit logic yet — timer callback is a no-op placeholder cleared by S5.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S4 modifies MarmotContext.tsx and registerHandlers.ts, both code files; files_code is non-empty.

mock_dependencies.description: Stub enqueueLeave from S2 resolved here — replaced with the real closure from MarmotContext

stories.name: auto-commit-path
stories.title: Auto-commit timer: Remove proposals + adminPubkeys update in a single commit
stories.description: Implement the debounce timer-fire handler in MarmotContext. Re-fetch live mlsGroup via clientRef.current?.groups.get(groupId). For each pending pubkey, look up leaf indexes via getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes; drop already-removed entries. Build plain-object Remove proposals ({ proposalType: PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE, remove: { removed: leafIndex } }). Compute remainingAdmins from mlsGroup.groupData?.adminPubkeys. Issue one mlsGroup.commit({ extraProposals: [...removeProposals, Proposals.proposeUpdateMetadata({ adminPubkeys: remainingAdmins })] }). On success clear committed entries; on failure leave entries in place for retry with freshly derived leaf indexes. Covers dual-admin race, stale-leaf guard, cold-start burst, and publish-fail retry semantics.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S5 modifies MarmotContext.tsx, a code file, and creates a new unit test file; files_code is non-empty and new_file = true.

stories.name: leave-announcement-rendering
stories.title: LeaveChatAnnouncement component, parseStructured extension, ChatBox branch, and i18n keys
stories.description: Extend StructuredContent union in parseStructured.ts with { type: 'leave_intent'; pubkey: string } and add the guard branch. Create the LeaveChatAnnouncement presentational component (gray sidebar, data-testid='leave-chat-announcement', uses useCopy for groups.leftGroup). Add the leave_intent branch in ChatBox.tsx's renderStructuredMessage resolving memberDisplay from profileMap with truncateNpub fallback. Add groups.leftGroup en and de entries to i18n.ts. Unit tests for parseStructured and i18n. No changes to ChatStoreContext.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S6 creates a new file (LeaveChatAnnouncement.tsx does not exist on disk) and modifies multiple code files (.ts, .tsx); new_file = true and files_code is non-empty.

stories.name: member-list-refresh-e2e
stories.title: Verify onMembersChanged fires on auto-remove commit and write e2e test
stories.description: Confirm that onMembersChanged at MarmotContext.tsx:599-609 fires when the auto-remove commit from S5 lands, causing the member list to refresh and drop the departed member. If existing wiring already handles this, this story is verification-only for that path. Covers the sole-admin and pre-admin-promotion no-op edge cases. Delivers the two-client Playwright e2e: User A creates group, invites User B; User B leaves; within 5s User A's client commits removal; User B's name disappears from User A's member list; User A can still send chat. Also asserts AC-ARCH boundary invariants as grep-based checks.
stories.lighter_path_reason: S7 creates a new e2e test file and may touch MarmotContext.tsx; new_file = true and files_code is non-empty.

dependency_notes: S1 (leaveSync.ts) is a prerequisite for S2 (handler needs parseLeaveIntent) and S3 (leaveGroup needs serialiseLeaveIntent + LEAVE_INTENT_KIND). S2 must precede S4 (S4 replaces the stub enqueueLeave wired in S2 with the real closure). S4 must precede S5 (S5 implements the timer body that S4 arms). S6 is independent — UI-only changes with no dependency on MLS state — placed at story_order 6 for serialized execution. S7 requires both S5 (auto-commit must exist) and S6 (e2e asserts the leave announcement renders) before e2e can pass. Execution order: S1 → S2 → S3 → S4 → S5 → S6 → S7.

invariants: enqueueLeave is called exactly once per well-formed kind-13 rumor received
enqueueLeave is never called for a rumor where parseLeaveIntent returns null
enqueueLeave receives the groupId from RumorHandlerContext and the pubkey from LeaveIntentPayload
serialiseLeaveIntent(p) produces JSON with exactly one key 'pubkey'
parseLeaveIntent(serialiseLeaveIntent(p)) returns {pubkey: p.pubkey} for any valid p
parseLeaveIntent returns null for any input where parsed.pubkey is not a string
pendingRemovalsRef.current is a Map<string, PendingRemoval[]> keyed by groupId
entries added during the commit window survive to the next timer tick
entries for successfully committed pubkeys are removed after commit
entries for pubkeys not found in the ratchet tree are dropped without commit
